page.title: Iron swords and iron sword inscriptions

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鉄剣・鉄刀銘文]]
'''Tetsuken/Tettō Meibun''' (鉄剣・鉄刀銘文) refers to textual inscriptions found on iron swords or blades. These inscribed swords are sometimes called '''Zaimēi Tetsuken''' (在銘鉄剣).<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=日本史必携 |date=2006-01-20 |year=2006 |publisher=吉川弘文館 |page=385}}</ref> This article discusses items excavated from Japanese [[kofun]] as well as the [[Nanatsusaya no Tachi]] preserved at [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] and the [[Heishi Shōrin Sword]] housed at [[Shitennō-ji]]. For reference, the term {{ill|Ken|en|Ken|qid=Q226769|lt=Ken|1=Ken}} refers to double-edged swords, while [[Japanese pagoda|Tō]] indicates single-edged blades. These artifacts serve as invaluable historical resources for understanding the [[Kofun period]].
== "Chūhei" Inscription Iron Sword from Tōdaijiyama Kofun == {{see also|Tōdaijiyama Kofun#Chūhei Inscription Sword}} [[File:Tōdaijiyama Kofun - Gold-inlaid Iron Sword.JPG|thumb|right|{{center|Gold-inlaid iron sword}}{{small|The "Chūhei" inscription (reversed) is visible on the lower part of the image. Tokyo National Museum display.}}]] The iron sword excavated in {{ill|1962|en|1962|qid=Q2764|lt=1962|1=1962}} (Showa 37) from [[Tōdaijiyama Kofun]] in [[Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], is 110 cm in length and features gold inlay inscriptions. The presumed text is as follows:
「'''中平□□ 五月丙午 造作支刀 百練清剛 上応星宿 □□□□'''」
== Nanatsusaya no Tachi at Isonokami Shrine == {{main|{{ill|8=ja|9=東大寺山古墳|Tōdaijiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}}}}
The [[Nanatsusaya no Tachi]] (Seven-Branched Sword) is traditionally believed to have been offered by the kingdom of [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] during the era of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} and preserved at [[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[Tenri, Nara Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,5272,98,220.html |title=コラム古代からのメッセージ 倭の五王の時代 七支刀の銘文 （№151）|publisher=藤井寺市|date=2009-06-04}}</ref> The sword, named for its unique form featuring six branch-like blades extending from its central body, contains a total of 61 gold-inlaid characters on its main body.
== "Ōshi" Inscription Iron Sword from Inaridai No.1 Kofun == [[File:「王賜」銘鉄剣 (複製).JPG|thumb|140px|right|{{center|Replica of the "Ōshi" Inscribed Iron Sword}}{{small|On display at the Ichihara City Cultural Heritage Center.}}]] {{seealso|Inaridai No.1 Kofun#Inscription}} The iron sword excavated from {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|稲荷台1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in [[Ichihara, Chiba Prefecture]], was found in the central wooden coffin of the kofun. The inscription reads:
* Obverse: 「'''王賜□□敬□（安）'''」 * Reverse: 「'''此廷□□□□'''」
This sword, likely dating to the mid-5th century, is believed to have been awarded by a "king" (likely one of the [[Five Kings of Wa]]) to a local warrior in recognition of their service.<ref>{{sfn|東野|2010|pp=12-13}}</ref>
== Silver-Inlaid Sword from Edafuneyama Kofun == [[File:Edafuneyama Kofun Silver-inlaid Sword.JPG|thumb|right|{{center|Silver-inlaid sword from Edafuneyama Kofun}}{{small|Tokyo National Museum display.}}]] This iron sword, featuring approximately 75 characters of silver inlay, was excavated from {{ill|Eta Funayama Kofun|en|Eta Funayama Kofun|qid=Q11259227|lt=Edafuneyama Kofun|1=Eta Funayama Kofun}} in [[Tamana, Kumamoto Prefecture]].<ref>{{sfn|江田船山国宝展実行委員会|2001}}</ref>
== Gold-Inlaid Iron Sword from Inariyama Kofun == {{Main|Inariyama Kofun Iron Sword}} {{Double image aside|right|Inariyama Sword (front).JPG|100|Inariyama Sword (back).JPG|100|{{center|Gold-inlaid iron sword (National Treasure) from [[Inariyama Kofun]]}}{{small|Displayed at the Saitama Prefectural Museum of History. Left: front; right: back.}}}} The gold-inlaid iron sword, bearing a 115-character inscription, was excavated from {{ill|Inariyama Kofun (Gyōda)|ja|稲荷山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inariyama Kofun (Gyōda)|1=Inariyama Kofun (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11596864}} in [[Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture]], in 1968.<ref>{{sfn|埼玉県教委|1979}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist|3}}
== Further Reading == * {{cite book|和書|author=東野治之|title=書の古代史|publisher=岩波書店|year=2010|ref={{sfnref|東野|2010}}}} * {{cite book|和書|author=江田船山国宝展実行委員会|title=江田船山国宝展|year=2001|ref={{sfnref|江田船山国宝展実行委員会|2001}}}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|鉄剣・鉄刀銘文|version=103612193|comment=Imported full ja history}}
